title: delphinium elatum subsp. nacladense
intro: Delphinium elatum subsp. nacladense, also known as the high mountain larkspur, is a subspecies of the perennial flowering plant Delphinium elatum, native to the high mountains of the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Delphinium elatum subsp. nacladense include its ability to treat muscle spasms, reduce inflammation, and prevent infection, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments. The herbal preparations of the plant typically involve drying and infusing the leaves and flowers, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal purposes.
